# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment for our batch processing jobs. The pods run data processing tasks and don't need to interact with the Kubernetes API. Use default service account settings for simplicity. Focus on getting the jobs running reliably.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: batch-processor
  labels:
    app: batch-processor
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: batch-processor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: batch-processor
    spec:
      # Use the default service account, but ensure its token is not mounted
      # as the application does not interact with the Kubernetes API.
      serviceAccountName: default
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      # Give the pod 60 seconds to gracefully terminate before being forcibly killed.
      # This is important for batch jobs to finish any in-flight processing.
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: processor
        image: your-repo/your-batch-job-image:latest # Replace with your actual image
        # Define resource requests and limits for reliable scheduling and to prevent resource exhaustion.
        # Adjust these values based on your job's actual requirements.
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "250m"   # Request 0.25 CPU core
            memory: "512Mi" # Request 512 MiB of memory
          limits:
            cpu: "1"      # Limit to 1 CPU core
            memory: "1Gi" # Limit to 1 GiB of memory
        # Example command and arguments if your container image requires them
        # If your image has a default ENTRYPOINT/CMD, you might not need these.
        # command: ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
        # args: ["/app/run_batch_job.sh; exit 0"]
        # For batch jobs, logging to stdout/stderr is crucial for Kubernetes to collect logs.
        # Configure your application to log to these streams.
      # RestartPolicy for a pod inside a Deployment is always 'Always' by default,
      # ensuring the container is restarted if it exits.
      restartPolicy: Always
